highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.149: Usage Breakdown, Scrollable Diffs & Markdown Rendering
highlights.summary: Version 2.1.149 adds a per-category breakdown to `/usage` so you can see exactly what's consuming your limits across skills, subagents, plugins, and MCP servers. The `/diff` detail view now supports full keyboard navigation (arrows, j/k, PgUp/PgDn, Space, Home/End), and Markdown output renders properly in the terminal. These quality-of-life improvements make it much easier to manage costs and review changes without leaving the CLI.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.147: Pinned Sessions & /code-review Command
highlights.summary: Pinned background sessions (created with Ctrl+T in `claude agents`) now stay alive when idle and survive Claude Code updates by restarting in place, making long-running agent workflows far more reliable. The `/simplify` command has been renamed to `/code-review` and now reports correctness bugs, giving you a dedicated command for quality checks on your code. If you relied on `/simplify`, update your muscle memory to `/code-review` right away.

highlights.title: Introducing Claude Opus 4.7
highlights.summary: Anthropic has announced Claude Opus 4.7, the latest and most capable model in the Opus family, which powers Claude Code's most demanding tasks. Developers using Claude Code can expect improved reasoning, better instruction-following, and stronger code generation when this model is selected. Check the Anthropic blog for details on availability and how to set Opus 4.7 as your preferred model in Claude Code.

highlights.title: Show HN: Spec-Driven Development Workflow for Claude Code
highlights.summary: This community-shared workflow proposes a structured approach to getting better results from Claude Code by first generating specs in multiple steps (requirements, code analysis, design) before breaking work into subtasks for implementation. The decomposition strategy prevents Claude from taking on too much at once, reducing hallucinations and incomplete outputs. Beginners especially benefit from this pattern because it mirrors good software engineering practice and gives Claude clear, bounded instructions.

highlights.title: Tell HN: Claude Code now allows Anthropic to remotely inject system prompts
highlights.summary: A Hacker News user claims to have discovered, while auditing the v2.1.150 binary, that Claude Code may now support remote system prompt injection by Anthropic — a significant concern for developers who rely on custom or patched system prompts. The thread is generating debate about transparency, trust, and the security implications of AI tooling with update-driven prompt changes. Developers should follow this discussion closely and review Anthropic's official documentation for clarification before drawing conclusions.

highlights.title: Complete Claude Code Course In 2 Hours For Developers
highlights.summary: This two-hour YouTube course is designed to take developers from zero to productive with Claude Code, covering setup, core commands, and real-world workflows. It's one of the most comprehensive free resources available and is well-suited for developers who prefer structured video learning over reading documentation. Bookmark this if you're just getting started or want a solid refresher on Claude Code fundamentals.

highlights.title: Claude Code Tutorial: Beginner to Advanced in 20 Minutes
highlights.summary: A fast-paced 20-minute video that walks through Claude Code from basic usage all the way to more advanced techniques, making it ideal for developers who want a quick but thorough orientation. The concise format is great for experienced developers who already understand coding fundamentals and just need to learn the Claude Code-specific tooling. Watch this if you want to get up to speed quickly without committing to a multi-hour course.

tips_of_the_day: Use `/usage` after each major coding session to spot which skills or MCP servers are consuming the most of your limits — then decide if those integrations are pulling their weight or can be disabled.

Before handing a large feature request to Claude Code, write a brief spec file (even just bullet points covering requirements and constraints) and ask Claude to read it first. This 'spec-driven' priming dramatically reduces mid-task confusion and backtracking.

Assign long-running or background tasks to a pinned session with Ctrl+T in `claude agents` — pinned sessions survive both idle periods and Claude Code updates, so your agents won't silently die while you're focused on other work.
